non_claim: This corpus proves that Ghost-Ark verification rejects these specific single-field mutations. It does not prove resistance to all attacks, does not prove cryptographic security of the primitives, and does not prove model safety, semantic truth, or compliance. All fixtures derive from synthetic test-vector receipts; nothing here contains prompts, completions, PII, secrets, tokens, credentials, or private keys.

attacks.mutation_description: Flips the final hex character of receipt_id so the id no longer matches the canonical unsigned receipt.
attacks.claim_boundary: receipt_id is derived, not asserted: a mutated id must fail recomputation.

attacks.mutation_description: Flips one hex character of the digestSha256 embedded in the signature envelope, leaving the receipt body and HMAC signature intact.
attacks.claim_boundary: The envelope digest must equal the recomputed canonical unsigned receipt digest even when the raw signature bytes still verify.

attacks.mutation_description: Flips the first byte of the decoded signature and re-encodes it as valid standard base64.
attacks.claim_boundary: A syntactically valid but wrong signature must fail cryptographic verification, not just format checks.

attacks.mutation_description: Replaces the envelope keyId with a different non-empty key identifier.
attacks.claim_boundary: The verifier must bind receipts to an expected key identity, not accept any self-declared keyId.

attacks.mutation_description: Replaces the immutable key ARN in a KMS-algorithm receipt envelope with a mutable KMS alias ARN.
attacks.claim_boundary: Mutable aliases are not accepted for signed evidence: alias retargeting would break key-identity binding.

attacks.mutation_description: Sets the envelope algorithm to KMS_SIGN_RSASSA_PSS_SHA_256 while the receipt signature_alg remains LOCAL_HMAC_SHA256_DEV_ONLY.
attacks.claim_boundary: Algorithm identity is part of the envelope contract; algorithm confusion between envelope and receipt must fail closed.

attacks.mutation_description: Rewrites the envelope schemaVersion to an unsupported value.
attacks.claim_boundary: Envelope schema versioning is enforced at verification, not only at signing. Before the strict envelope check this mutation verified end-to-end.

attacks.mutation_description: Adds an extra attacker-controlled field to the signature envelope.
attacks.claim_boundary: The envelope field set is exact; smuggled fields must fail closed. Before the strict envelope check this mutation verified end-to-end.

attacks.mutation_description: Removes keyId from the signature envelope.
attacks.claim_boundary: All five envelope fields are required; a missing field must fail closed.

attacks.mutation_description: Re-encodes the canonical envelope JSON as padded standard base64 instead of unpadded base64url. Node's lenient decoder still parses it, so without the strict envelope check this mutation verified end-to-end.
attacks.claim_boundary: receipt_signature encoding is exact (unpadded base64url); alternative encodings of the same bytes must fail closed.

attacks.mutation_description: Replaces receipt_signature with text that is not base64url and does not decode to JSON.
attacks.claim_boundary: Garbage envelopes must be rejected as malformed, never silently treated as unsigned.

attacks.mutation_description: Flips one hex character of prev_receipt_hash in a chained receipt, attempting to re-point the hash chain.
attacks.claim_boundary: Chain linkage is signature-bound: rewriting history must break receipt identity and digest binding.

attacks.mutation_description: Flips one hex character of tenant_id_hash, attempting to reassign the receipt to a different tenant identity.
attacks.claim_boundary: Tenant identity is signature-bound inside the receipt; tenant reassignment must fail closed.

attacks.mutation_description: The receipt is a byte-identical copy of a valid tenant-repro-a receipt, presented to a consumer that expects tenant-repro-b. verifyDecisionReceipt alone passes; the tenant expectation must be enforced by the consumer.
attacks.claim_boundary: Documented boundary: verifyDecisionReceipt does not check tenant expectations. A cryptographically valid receipt from tenant A must still be rejected by a tenant-B consumer comparing tenant_id_hash.

attacks.mutation_description: Duplicates the first action_taken entry. Receipt v1 preserves array multiplicity and order in the canonical form, so inserting a duplicate changes the signed bytes.
attacks.claim_boundary: action_taken multiplicity is semantically significant in receipt v1 and is signature-bound; mutation requires a schema migration, not a silent rewrite.

attacks.mutation_description: Attaches the intact, correctly signed envelope from hmac-chained onto the hmac-baseline receipt body (signature transplant).
attacks.claim_boundary: A signature is bound to one canonical payload; transplanting a valid envelope onto a different receipt must fail closed.

attacks.mutation_description: Flips one hex character of input_digest, attempting to re-bind the receipt to a different input.
attacks.claim_boundary: Input binding is by digest; changing the claimed input must break receipt identity, digest, and signature checks.

attacks.mutation_description: Drops the last retrieved_context_digests entry, attempting to hide one retrieved context item from the signed record.
attacks.claim_boundary: Retrieved-context provenance is signature-bound; removing a context digest must fail closed.

attacks.mutation_description: Removes the required policy_hash field entirely, attempting to strip the policy binding from the receipt.
attacks.claim_boundary: policy_hash is a required schema field: a receipt without a policy binding must fail schema validation, never default to an unbounded policy.

attacks.mutation_description: Replaces the execution_nonce with a different well-formed nonce, attempting to re-bind the receipt to a different invocation (replay-style re-pointing).
attacks.claim_boundary: The execution nonce binds one invocation; re-pointing it must break receipt identity, digest, and signature checks. This fixture covers nonce binding inside the signed receipt, not runtime replay prevention, which is enforced by the nonce store.

attacks.mutation_description: Shifts the receipt timestamp by one second while leaving every other field and the signature intact.
attacks.claim_boundary: The timestamp is signature-bound; back- or forward-dating a receipt must fail closed. This does not prove the original timestamp was accurate, only that it cannot be silently rewritten.

attacks.mutation_description: Rewrites request_id to a value containing non-ASCII text (a precomposed é). Both verifiers must canonicalize the non-ASCII bytes identically and reject via the same recomputed receipt id.
attacks.claim_boundary: Non-ASCII string content must hash identically across independent verifier implementations. The differential test asserts the Node and Python verifiers recompute the same expected receipt id for this fixture; canonicalization performs no Unicode normalization, so NFC and NFD spellings are distinct signed bytes.

attacks.mutation_description: Adds an unsigned top-level field to an otherwise valid, correctly signed receipt (unsigned-claim smuggling).
attacks.claim_boundary: Unknown top-level receipt fields are rejected, not silently stripped. Before the strict schema, validation stripped unknown fields and this mutation verified end-to-end, so a reader of the raw JSON could be shown unsigned attacker-controlled content next to a PASS verdict.

attacks.mutation_description: A truncated copy of the valid baseline receipt: the file is not parseable JSON.
attacks.claim_boundary: Malformed receipt documents must be rejected at parse time, never partially interpreted or treated as unsigned.

attacks.mutation_description: Removes receipt_signature entirely, presenting an unsigned receipt as if it were verifiable.
attacks.claim_boundary: An unsigned receipt is not a receipt: a missing signature envelope must fail schema validation, never degrade to unsigned acceptance.

attacks.mutation_description: Rewrites schema_version to ghost.receipt.v999, an unsupported version, while leaving the rest of the receipt intact.
attacks.claim_boundary: Verifiers fail closed on unknown receipt schema versions instead of guessing forward-compatible semantics.
